data.title: As a DSO orchestrator, error-reactive fallback detects rate-limit errors in sub-agent results
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Pattern-match rate-limit keywords in sub-agent result text and trigger throttling when detected
**Why**: Secondary safety net when the OAuth cache is stale or the endpoint is unreachable
**Scope**:
- IN: Pattern matching for rate.limit|usage.limit|quota.exceeded in sub-agent results, integration with agent-batch-lifecycle.sh throttle state, throttle to MAX_AGENTS: 1 on match
- OUT: OAuth polling (Story 2), pre-check protocol (Story 3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, injecting a rate-limit error string into a mock sub-agent result triggers throttle to MAX_AGENTS: 1 for subsequent batches
  ← Satisfies: "error-reactive fallback detects rate-limit errors in sub-agent results and triggers throttling"
- When this story is complete, non-quota 429 errors (transient API rate limits) do not trigger the fallback — only quota-specific keywords match
  ← Satisfies: "error-reactive fallback detects rate-limit errors"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Considerations

- [Testing] Pattern matching on unstructured text is inherently brittle — use minimal keyword set and document what each pattern detects
- [Reliability] Fallback only affects future batches — in-flight agents from the current batch cannot be recalled. This temporal gap is by design; the fallback prevents escalation, not immediate recovery
- [Reliability] Error-reactive and pre-check can temporarily disagree — pre-check said unlimited, error-reactive says throttle. Define: error-reactive overrides pre-check verdict for NEXT batch only
